metadata.seed: Anyone ever have there SO push them away from depression to have them come back and continue to have a relationship? I’m in this situation now, and I want her back, but I want her to be happy with herself, and be able to deal with her depression even on the hardest days. Just didn’t know if anyone else was ever in this situation.

patient.query: Anyone ever have there SO push them away from depression to have them come back and continue to have a relationship? I’m in this situation now, and I want her back, but I want her to be happy with herself, and be able to deal with her depression even on the hardest days. Just didn’t know if anyone else was ever in this situation.

llm_response.response: It’s not uncommon for people to try to “push” a partner out of a depressive slump only to find that the relationship ends up hurting both of you. The healthiest approach is to focus on being a supportive friend first—encourage her to seek professional help, listen without judgment, and set clear boundaries so you don’t become a substitute for therapy. If she’s willing to work on herself, you can gradually re‑establish a relationship built on mutual respect and shared coping strategies, but remember that her recovery is ultimately her own responsibility. If you’re unsure how to navigate this, talking to a therapist or a support group can give you both clearer guidance.
